label: 1211_ferc_hydropower_hydroelectric_hydroelectric project
description: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission regulates hydroelectric projects under the Federal Power Act. Legislation proposed changes to permit FERC to extend certain permits and authorize stays for specific projects, impacting energy regulatory processes.

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 5892, Hydropower Regulatory Efficiency Act of 2012
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Energy and Commerce on June 20, 2012 Under the Federal Power Act, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) issues licenses and regulates hydroelectric facilities, regardless of size. H.R. 5892 would amend current law to allow FERC to extend certain permits related…

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 460, Bonneville Unit Clean Hydropower Facilitation Act
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Natural Resources on April 25, 2012 CBO expects that enacting H.R. 460 would lead to the development of hydropower facilities at the Diamond Fork Project in Utah by a nonfederal entity within a few years, sooner than expected under current law. Based on information from…

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 316, Collinsville Renewable Energy Promotion Act
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Energy and Commerce on January 22, 2013 H.R. 316 would authorize the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to reinstate the licenses and extend the deadline for beginning construction of two hydroelectric projects (numbers 10822 and 10823) in Hartford County,…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 4045, Hydropower Clean Energy Future Act
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 4045 would amend timelines and procedures regarding the licensing of nonfederal hydropower projects. Additionally, the bill would: Require federal agencies to procure an increasing amount of their electricity from renewable energy sources in future years, culminating at a minimum of 25 percent in 2028 and beyond;…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 1607, a bill to clarify jurisdiction with respect to certain Bureau of Reclamation pumped storage development, and for other purposes
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 1607 would direct the Forest Service to withdraw land, which CBO expects would total about 17,000 acres, from the National Forest System in an area adjacent to the Salt River Project (SRP) in Arizona and transfer it to the Bureau of Reclamation (BOR). The bill also would clarify that regulatory jurisdiction to…

newest_articles.title: S. 3450, Sun River Hydropower Authorization Act
newest_articles.excerpt: S. 3450 would authorize the Bureau of Reclamation (BOR) to develop hydropower generation facilities through a lease with a private entity in Montana’s Sun River project. Under that agreement, private entities would pay BOR fees based on the amount of energy produced by the project; those fees could be spent or applied…

central_articles.title: S. 1751, Bureau of Reclamation Pumped Storage Hydropower Development Act
central_articles.excerpt: Under current law, nonfederal entities that propose to develop hydropower at reservoirs administered by the Bureau of Reclamation (BOR) must enter into a lease with BOR or obtain a license from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC). The regulatory jurisdiction of each BOR reservoir was previously negotiated…
